The development of Shree Pundalik occurred in the following order:
  1. First Shree Pundalik: In the year 1953, Shri Pundalik was established as a small temple in a rented premises. The deity was worshipped and the temple was managed by Shree Pundalik Sevalaya, a trust established by Late Shri. R. S. Sane, a renowned businessman of the city.
  2. Second

Shree Pundalik originates from the ancient city of Pandharpur in Maharashtra, India. It is one of the most revered pilgrimage sites in India. The story of Shree Pundalik is deeply connected with the history and culture of Maharashtra and the state’s patron deity, Vithal or Vithobha. The temple is a significant cultural and religious landmark, attracting millions of devotees each year from all over India and the world.
Shree Pundalik

The development of Shree Pundalik occurred in the 13th century in the city of Pandarapura in present-day Karnataka. This temple is dedicated to the god Shri Vittal, a manifestation of Lord Vithal (Krishna).
Shri Vittal is the presiding deity of the city of Pandarapura and is revered by devotees across the state for his healing powers and benevolence. Shri Vittal is depicted in a standing posture,

The development of Shree Pundalik occurred in the 18th century, with the help of Shri. Narayanrao Patwardhan and Shri. Vasantrao Patwardhan, who were great devotees of Lord Pundalik. They were instrumental in establishing the temple in 1786 and also in the construction of the Samadhi of Lord Pundarika. The temple was later renovated by Shri. Gopalrao Patwardhan

Where Shree Pundalik is from, people speak the language of the gods, and their songs echo with the rhythm of nature. The land itself is a poem that tells a tale of ancient wisdom and mystic secrets. Shree Pundalik's journey is an odyssey of self-discovery, a quest to unravel the mysteries of the universe and find the meaning of life. Shree Pundalik originates from the 14th-century text, Pundalika Charitra. The legend revolves around a devout devotee, Shree Pundalik, who is blessed by Lord Krishna. The temple is a blend of ancient and medieval styles, showcasing the evolution of Indian temple architecture over the centuries.

Where Shree Pundalik is from, people speak the language of Marathi, which has been in existence for centuries. The city has a rich history dating back to the 12th century when it was ruled by the Yadava Dynasty. The city has seen the rise and fall of several dynasties, including the Rashtrakuta, Shilahara, and Yadava dynasties. Shree Pundalik is located at the confluence of the Krishna and Bh

Shree Pundalik originates from Maharashtra in India and is a Maratha saint who lived around 800 AD. He is considered as a devotee of Lord Vitthal, a deity of the Hindu god Krishna. He is also believed by the devotees to be a manifestation of Lord Krishna himself. Shree Pundalik is revered as the first devotee of Lord Vitthal.

Where Shree Pundalik is from, people speak the language of their ancestors, which is Marathi.
Marathi is a language that has its roots in Sanskrit and other ancient languages like Prakrit and Ardha Magadhi.
It is a part of the Indo-European language family and has a rich vocabulary that includes words from other languages like Sanskrit, Hindi, and Urdu.
